action.reasoning: Observation: Best is still iter 8 (0.0462) with 3-seed CSRankNorm. The 7-seed diverse params actually did worse on IC. Analysis: Diversification with extreme params hurt. The simple 3-seed CSRankNorm config worked best. Let me restore that exact recipe but add tweaks: try Huber loss or just retrain with the original 3-seed config + maybe ensemble across CSRankNorm and CSZScoreNorm models to combine their strengths. Actually best approach: revert to the proven 3-seed CSRankNorm config and try larger ensemble of similar (not divergent) seeds. Also try adding feature engineering or try MSE on raw labels with z-score per cross-section. Decision: Keep things simple - revert to 3-seed CSRankNorm with default params (which scored 0.0462). Use 5 seeds with same params, only varying random_state to keep diversity through bootstrap not parameter changes.
